summary: Conceptual demonstration of WebSocket CSRF vulnerability. The CheckOrigin function unconditionally returns true, allowing any website to establish WebSocket connections. Full exploitation requires browser interaction.
expected_security_impact: Any cross-origin website should be able to establish WebSocket connection to CometBFT node and perform operations.
actual_observed_result: The vulnerable code (ws_handler.go:49: CheckOrigin returns true) allows all cross-origin requests. Conceptual PoC demonstrates the attack scenario. Full PoC would require browser environment.
verification_method: Code review confirms CheckOrigin function always returns true. The conceptual PoC explains the attack scenario and impact.
evidence_produced_by_run: CheckOrigin: func(*http.Request) bool { return true } confirmed
limitations_or_blockers: Full PoC requires browser environment to demonstrate actual WebSocket connection from cross-origin webpage
Victim interaction (visiting malicious website) needed for complete exploit chain

reproduction.source_acquisition: Conceptual demonstration based on code review of CometBFT repository
